root_text.sn15.4:1.3: Pubbā koṭi na paññāyati avijjānīvaraṇānaṁ sattānaṁ taṇhāsaṁyojanānaṁ sandhāvataṁ saṁsarataṁ.
root_text.sn15.4:1.4: Taṁ kiṁ maññatha, bhikkhave, katamaṁ nu kho bahutaraṁ, yaṁ vā vo iminā dīghena addhunā sandhāvataṁ saṁsarataṁ mātuthaññaṁ pītaṁ, yaṁ vā catūsu mahāsamuddesu udakan”ti?
root_text.sn15.4:1.5: “Yathā kho mayaṁ, bhante, bhagavatā dhammaṁ desitaṁ ājānāma, etadeva, bhante, bahutaraṁ yaṁ no iminā dīghena addhunā sandhāvataṁ saṁsarataṁ mātuthaññaṁ pītaṁ, na tveva catūsu mahāsamuddesu udakan”ti.
root_text.sn15.4:2.1: “Sādhu sādhu, bhikkhave, sādhu kho me tumhe, bhikkhave, evaṁ dhammaṁ desitaṁ ājānātha.
root_text.sn15.4:2.2: Etadeva, bhikkhave, bahutaraṁ yaṁ vo iminā dīghena addhunā sandhāvataṁ saṁsarataṁ mātuthaññaṁ pītaṁ, na tveva catūsu mahāsamuddesu udakaṁ.

translation_text.sn15.4:1.2: “Mendicants, this transmigration has no known beginning.
translation_text.sn15.4:1.3: No first point is found of sentient beings roaming and transmigrating, shrouded by ignorance and fettered by craving.
translation_text.sn15.4:1.4: What do you think? Which is more: the mother’s milk you’ve drunk while roaming and transmigrating for such a very long time, or the water in the four oceans?”
translation_text.sn15.4:1.5: “As we understand the Buddha’s teaching, the mother’s milk we’ve drunk while roaming and transmigrating is more than the water in the four oceans.”
translation_text.sn15.4:2.1: “Good, good, mendicants! It’s good that you understand my teaching like this.
translation_text.sn15.4:2.2: The mother’s milk you’ve drunk while roaming and transmigrating for such a very long time is more than the water in the four oceans.
translation_text.sn15.4:2.4: This transmigration has no known beginning. …
translation_text.sn15.4:2.5: This is quite enough for you to become disillusioned, dispassionate, and freed regarding all conditions.”
